name: Cerebras Systems Inc.
business: Cerebras Systems sells AI infrastructure, including wafer-scale processors (WSE) and supercomputers for training and inference, delivered via on-premises deployments, cloud services, and partner clouds to hyperscalers, foundation model labs, enterprises, and sovereign AI initiatives.

exposures.sentence: We are building the fastest AI infrastructure in the world.
At the core of our solution is the Cerebras WSE, the largest and fastest AI processor ever brought to market in high volumes.
We expect to significantly increase our investments in cloud infrastructure and data centers to enable growth in our inference service.
Our on-premises customers include large enterprises, national laboratories, the U.S. Department of Defense, and Sovereign AI initiatives.

catalysts.event: Signed binding term sheet with AWS to deploy Cerebras in AWS data centers
catalysts.sentence: In March 2026, we signed a term sheet with AWS, under which AWS will become the first hyperscaler to deploy Cerebras in its own data centers.

catalysts.event: Entered into Master Revenue Agreement (MRA) with OpenAI
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we entered into the MRA with OpenAI, which represents a substantial portion of our projected revenues over the next several years.

tamClaims.claim: The combined market for AI training infrastructure and our addressable market within AI inference is estimated to be $251 billion in 2025 and is expected to grow to $672 billion by 2029 a 28% CAGR, according to Bloomberg Intelligence.
tamClaims.sentence: The combined market for AI training infrastructure and our addressable market within AI inference is estimated to be $251 billion in 2025 and is expected to grow to $672 billion by 2029 a 28% CAGR, according to Bloomberg Intelligence.

tamClaims.claim: According to Bloomberg Intelligence, our addressable market within the AI inference market is expected to grow from approximately $66 billion in 2025 to $292 billion by 2029, a 45% CAGR.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Bloomberg Intelligence, our addressable market within the AI inference market is expected to grow from approximately $66 billion in 2025 to $292 billion by 2029, a 45% CAGR.

tamClaims.claim: According to Dell Oro Group, worldwide data center infrastructure capital expenditures are expected to grow from $679 billion in 2025 to $1.7 trillion by 2030, representing a 21% CAGR.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Dell Oro Group, worldwide data center infrastructure capital expenditures are expected to grow from $679 billion in 2025 to $1.7 trillion by 2030, representing a 21% CAGR.

tamClaims.claim: The AI training infrastructure market is expected to grow from approximately $185 billion in 2025 to $380 billion by 2029, a 20% CAGR, according to Bloomberg Intelligence.
tamClaims.sentence: The AI training infrastructure market is expected to grow from approximately $185 billion in 2025 to $380 billion by 2029, a 20% CAGR, according to Bloomberg Intelligence.
